scheme_name: One Act Play competition at High School, Higher Secondary School and College level (Konkani, Marathi & English)
page_title: One Act Play competition at High School, Higher Secondary School and College level (Konkani, Marathi & English)

details: The scheme "One Act Play Competition at High School, Higher Secondary School, and College Level (Konkani, Marathi & English)" is a Cultural Scheme by the Department of Art, Culture and Museums, Government of Goa, aimed at encouraging personality development through theatrical skills among students by providing a platform for stage performance, where entries are invited via press notes, all educational institutions in Goa are eligible, plays are staged as per the Kala Academy programme, and participating institutions receive transport reimbursement as per government norms.
benefits: Amount of Prizes At High School Level
Consolation Prize (2): Rs. 2,000/- each (Marathi, Konkani, English - Subject to more than ten entries received)
1st Prize (Male & Female): Rs. 1,000/- each (Marathi, Konkani, English);
2nd Prize (Male & Female): Rs. 500/- each (Marathi, Konkani, English)
Best Stage Setting: Rs. 1,000/- (Marathi, Konkani, English);
Best Background Music: Rs. 1,000/- (Marathi, Konkani, English);
Amount of Prizes At Higher Secondary/College Level
Consolation Prize (2): Rs. 3,000/- each (Marathi, Konkani, English - Subject to more than ten entries received)
1st Prize (Male & Female): Rs. 2,000/- each (Marathi, Konkani, English);
2nd Prize (Male & Female): Rs. 1,000/- each (Marathi, Konkani, English)
Best Stage Setting: Rs. 2,000/- (Marathi, Konkani, English);
Best Background Music: Rs. 2,000/- (Marathi, Konkani, English);
*A free auditorium with necessary infrastructure is provided as per Kala Academy rules.
eligibility: Educational institutions (including High Schools, Higher Secondary Schools, and Colleges) from Goa are eligible.

offline: The prescribed format of the application form is provided in the entries invited by the Kala Academy by releasing press note in local dailies.

In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the
Member Secretary, Kala Academy, Panaji, Goa
Member Secretary/ Chairman, Kala Academy, Goa.
* The competition event is also declared in local dailies.

documents_required: Any other documents as specified in the rules and regulations provided by Kala Academy.

faqs.question: What types of educational institutions are eligible to participate in the one act play competition?
faqs.answer: All High Schools, Higher Secondary Schools, and Colleges from the State of Goa are eligible to participate in the competition.

faqs.question: In which languages can High School students perform their one act plays?
faqs.answer: High School students can perform in Konkani, English, or Marathi as per the competition guidelines.

faqs.question: In which languages can Higher Secondary School and College students perform their one act plays?
faqs.answer: Higher Secondary School and College students can perform in Konkani, Marathi, or English as per the competition rules.

faqs.question: Where can interested institutions obtain the entry forms and competition rules?
faqs.answer: Entry forms and rules are available free of charge at Kala Academy once the event is announced in local newspapers.

faqs.question: Is there an entry fee for participating in the one act play competition?
faqs.answer: Yes, a nominal entry fee of ₹100/- is required per participating institution, regardless of the number of plays submitted.

faqs.question: Does the competition provide any financial support for transportation?
faqs.answer: Yes, participating institutions receive to-and-fro transport charges as per government rates for one vehicle to carry stage materials and artists.

faqs.question: What kind of venue facilities are provided for the competition?
faqs.answer: An auditorium with permissible infrastructure is provided free of charge as per Kala Academy’s rules.

faqs.question: What is the prize money for the best performance in the High School Marathi section?
faqs.answer: The first prize is ₹7,000/-, the second prize is ₹5,000/-, and the third prize is ₹3,000/-.

faqs.question: Are there consolation prizes in the High School English section, and what are the conditions?
faqs.answer: Yes, two consolation prizes of ₹2,000/- each are awarded if more than ten entries are received in the section.

faqs.question: What prizes are given for best individual performances at the High School level?
faqs.answer: The first prize for best male and female performers is ₹1,000/- each, and the second prize is ₹500/- each.

faqs.question: Are there special awards for technical aspects like stage setting and costumes?
faqs.answer: Yes, awards of ₹1,000/- each are given for best stage setting, costumes, background music, lighting, and make-up.

faqs.question: How does the prize structure differ for Higher Secondary/College level participants?
faqs.answer: Prizes are higher, with the best performance in each section receiving ₹10,000/-, ₹7,000/-, and ₹5,000/- for first, second, and third places.

faqs.question: Are there awards for technical excellence at the College level?
faqs.answer: Yes, awards of ₹2,000/- each are given for best stage setting, costumes, background music, lighting, and make-up.

faqs.question: Who can be contacted for grievance redressal regarding the competition?
faqs.answer: The Member Secretary or Chairman of Kala Academy, Goa, can address any grievances related to the competition.
